title: OOF Empirical-Bayes Stint Hazard Priors
summary: The current best code is rich in static tyre-window heuristics, but recent F1 and survival literature points toward latent degradation plus sequential event-risk estimation.
rationale: The current best code is rich in static tyre-window heuristics, but recent F1 and survival literature points toward latent degradation plus sequential event-risk estimation. A fold-local hazard prior can summarize how pit risk rises with stint age, compound, and laps remaining without fitting a full survival model.
implementation_hint: For each CV fold, build shrinkage-smoothed hazard tables for P(pit next lap | compound, TyreLife bin, laps_remaining bin, stint, slick/wet, race/regime). Use only training rows, shrink race-specific tables toward global tables, then add prior logit, cumulative hazard, and residual-vs-global hazard features to the main booster.
expected_effect: Better ranking on borderline pit-window rows, especially when raw TyreLife ratios are ambiguous but historical stop-risk shape is informative.
risk: Easy leakage if hazard tables are computed on all rows or with same-race future laps. Must be strictly fold-local and chronologically causal within race.
